steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows a comma after the independent clause "this period...is remembered for its grandeur and artistry." The correct choice must not create a second independent clause, which would produce a comma splice. The decisive clue is the word "its" before the blank: "its vehicles ______ by collectors" can form either a finite clause or a nonfinite phrase depending on the verb form chosen.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'are highly prized': The finite present-tense verb "are" makes "its vehicles are highly prized by collectors" a full independent clause; joining it to the first clause with only a comma creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'had been highly prized': The finite past-perfect verb "had been" likewise creates an independent clause after the comma, producing a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — 'highly prized': The nonfinite past participle "highly prized" forms an absolute phrase ("its vehicles highly prized by collectors") that modifies the main clause without creating a second independent clause — no comma splice. ✓
  • Choice D — 'were highly prized': The finite past-tense verb "were" creates an independent clause after the comma, producing a comma splice. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
